meta.title: Create VM: System NAS | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.description: Create NAS virtual machines on Proxmox VE with ProxMenux. Supports Synology DSM (loader), TrueNAS SCALE / CORE, OpenMediaVault, XigmaNAS, Rockstor and ZimaOS. Umbrel OS is available via community script.
meta.ogTitle: Create VM: System NAS | ProxMenux Documentation
meta.ogDescription: Create NAS virtual machines on Proxmox VE with ProxMenux. Supports Synology DSM (loader), TrueNAS SCALE / CORE, OpenMediaVault, XigmaNAS, Rockstor and ZimaOS.

header.title: Create VM: System NAS
header.description: ProxMenux supports seven NAS operating systems as dedicated VMs, plus an optional Umbrel OS path via a community script. Most systems share the same auto-ISO flow; Synology DSM uses a specialised loader wizard.

intro.title: How the NAS selector works
intro.body: The NAS selector detects the latest stable release of each appliance (querying its upstream mirror), downloads the ISO — or builds the VM from a pre-packaged image — and then hands over to the generic ProxMenux VM wizard for CPU, RAM, disk and network configuration.

cards.name: Synology DSM
cards.tagline: DiskStation Manager with the ARC / RR loader. Requires configuring the loader before first boot.

cards.name: TrueNAS SCALE
cards.tagline: Debian-based TrueNAS with Docker / Kubernetes apps and ZFS as primary filesystem.

cards.name: TrueNAS CORE
cards.tagline: FreeBSD-based TrueNAS. Enterprise-grade ZFS storage with jails and plugins.

cards.name: OpenMediaVault
cards.tagline: Lightweight Debian-based NAS with a modular plugin architecture. Great for older hardware.

cards.name: XigmaNAS
cards.tagline: FreeBSD-based NAS — the original successor to FreeNAS, focused on ZFS and simplicity.

cards.name: Rockstor
cards.tagline: openSUSE-based NAS built around Btrfs. Rock-ons (Docker apps) extend functionality.

cards.name: ZimaOS
cards.tagline: Lightweight NAS OS focused on media, Docker apps and home automation. Uses its own installer.

umbrel.title: Community option: Umbrel OS
umbrel.bodyRich: The 8th entry in the NAS menu is Umbrel OS. It runs a community-scripts installer maintained outside ProxMenux, so it is not documented here. The installer handles the entire lifecycle and prints its default credentials at the end.

zfsMem.title: ZFS memory rule of thumb
zfsMem.bodyRich: If you pick a ZFS-based NAS (TrueNAS, XigmaNAS), allocate at least 8 GB RAM to the VM — ZFS uses free memory as ARC cache and performance degrades noticeably below that. Rockstor (Btrfs) and OMV (ext4) are far less memory-hungry.

items.label: Synology VM
items.tail: — dedicated installer for Synology DSM (Xpenology) on Proxmox.

items.label: Other System NAS
items.tail: — TrueNAS, OpenMediaVault, Rockstor, XigmaNAS and similar.

items.label: Import Disk to VM
items.tail: — pass real physical disks to the NAS VM (recommended over virtual disks for production).

items.label: ZFS Management commands
items.tail: — useful if your NAS distribution uses ZFS underneath.
